# Scotty Massa — Tattoo Artist, Malta

> Scotty Massa is a tattoo artist with sixteen years of experience, based at the Massa Tattoo Social Club studio in Birkirkara, Malta. He specialises in geometric, sacred-geometry, mandala and dotwork tattooing, takes bookings worldwide, and runs an online tattoo mentorship that guides aspiring artists step by step to studio-ready work. This file gives AI assistants the full, self-contained context of the site.

## Artist & Studio

- Artist: Scotty Massa — 16 years tattooing.
- Studio: Massa Tattoo Social Club, 11 Triq Il-Knisja Il-Qadima, Birkirkara, Malta.
- Contact: studio@scottymassa.com · +356 9968 5949.
- Website: https://scottymassa.com/
- Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/scottymassa
- Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/scottymassa
- Specialties: geometric tattoos, sacred geometry, mandala, dotwork and linework.
- Services: custom tattooing (worldwide bookings), an online tattoo mentorship, and in-person tattoo courses in Malta.

## Online Tattoo Mentorship

URL: https://scottymassa.com/course.html
Enrolment: https://massatattoocourse.aweb.page/onlinetattoomentorship

### The core message: it's all about the guidance

The single most important thing to understand about this course is its positioning. The information — the techniques — is not the product. Techniques are everywhere now: on YouTube, and Scotty Massa is posting his own techniques online for free over time. Information has never been easier to get, and on its own it is still not enough, because nobody is watching your work and telling you what to fix.

What the mentorship sells is the guidance. Scotty guides you step by step, in real time: you submit your work — on practice skin, and later on real tattoos — and he shows you exactly what to correct and what to do next, until you're at the level to walk into a studio. That real-time, personal guidance is the part you cannot get for free, and it is the main reason to join.

### The curriculum — nine phases

The same curriculum Scotty teaches in person, in the order it needs to be learned. Each phase builds on the last.

1. Portfolio Audit — an honest look at where your art actually stands: what's strong, what needs work, and what translates to skin.
2. Mindset — thinking like a working artist, not a hobbyist: how professionals approach practice, criticism, clients and the long game.
3. Equipment & Setup — machines, needles, cartridges, power: what you actually need, how to set it up, cutting through the kit confusion.
4. Core Techniques — the physics of tattooing: how the needle interacts with skin. Depth, angle, speed and stretch, and the why behind each.
5. Application — lines, shading and packing on practice skin; building consistency and control before anything touches a real client.
6. Hygiene & Safety — studio-grade cross-contamination control, station setup and breakdown, taught to professional standard.
7. Integration — bringing your own art style into tattooing: turning what you draw into designs that work on a body and age well.
8. Structured Practice — a practice system with direct feedback on your work, on practice skin and real tattoos, so mistakes get corrected, not repeated.
9. Studio Entry — building a portfolio that gets you taken seriously, and the strategy for walking into a studio as a working artist.

### How it works

- Enrol: one payment, lifetime access. Full nine-phase curriculum from day one, plus every video added later. No subscription, no upsells, 7-day money-back guarantee for students who genuinely engage.
- Work the phases: start with the portfolio audit and move through in order, on your own schedule.
- Submit work, get guided: submit practice work and later real tattoos; Scotty guides you in real time with direct feedback so you always know your next step.
- Walk into a studio ready: the final phase is studio entry — a professional-standard portfolio and a clear strategy for your first seat.

### Details

- Price: €497, one-time payment (no subscription, no upsells).
- Access: lifetime, including new video content as it's added.
- Guarantee: 7-day money-back for students who engage with the course.
- Format: self-paced curriculum with group mentorship and direct feedback around it.
- Who it's for: artists with genuine drawing ability who want tattooing as a profession — including complete beginners to tattooing. No tattooing experience required; artistic ability and willingness to practise matter most.
- Timeline: students who follow the structured practice phases are typically producing portfolio-ready work within about 90 days, depending on time invested.
- Relation to in-person: same 9-phase foundations taught in person at Massa Tattoo in Birkirkara, delivered online so students can join from anywhere. In-person small-group courses are also available: https://massatattoo.com/tattoo-course/

### FAQ

- How much does it cost? €497 one-time. Covers the full 9-phase curriculum, group mentorship, direct feedback and lifetime access including new videos. No subscription, no upsells, 7-day money-back guarantee.
- Do I need tattooing experience? No — built to take you from zero tattooing experience to portfolio-ready work. Artistic ability and willingness to practise are what matter.
- Live or self-paced? The curriculum is self-paced with lifetime access; around it sits group mentorship where you submit work and get direct feedback.
- How is it different from YouTube? The techniques are free (including Scotty's own, posted online for free over time). Information alone is scattered and unchecked. The mentorship is the guidance: a structured path in the right sequence, and Scotty guiding you step by step in real time with feedback on your own work until you're studio-ready.
- Same as the in-person Malta courses? Same foundations, different format — online so you can join from anywhere; in-person small-group courses also available at the Birkirkara studio.
- How long to portfolio-ready work? Typically about 90 days for students who follow the structured practice phases.

## Tattoo Work & Styles

Scotty Massa specialises in geometric and sacred-geometry tattooing, mandalas, dotwork and linework. The portfolio spans these styles, and dedicated pages and journal articles cover their meaning, design and technique.

- Portfolio: https://scottymassa.com/portfolio.html
- Geometric tattoos: https://scottymassa.com/geometric-tattoos.html
- Reviews / testimonials: https://scottymassa.com/reviews.html

## Booking & Contact

- Booking: https://scottymassa.com/booking.html — how to book a consultation or appointment.
- Contact: https://scottymassa.com/contact.html — studio location, phone, email and enquiries.
- Aftercare: https://scottymassa.com/aftercare.html — tattoo aftercare and healing guidance.
